response.justification: Sarah Smith is a seasoned full-stack web and backend engineer with over 15 years of experience, but her background is fundamentally misaligned with this role. Her C++ exposure is limited to approximately six months at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a bachelor's thesis project — far below the depth required for architecting and delivering high-performance industrial C++ systems. Her career has been almost entirely TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang focused, with no evidence of multi-threaded systems programming, message-broker-based architectures at industrial scale, MQTT, IEC 62443 compliance, or any industrial automation domain knowledge.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship foundation: consistent evidence of TDD, CI/CD, agile practices, and mentoring across multiple roles
Event-driven and distributed systems exposure through GISCollective platform and high-throughput micro-services work at Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP requests/sec)
Demonstrated ability to ramp up on new domains quickly (e.g., took over iOS/Apple TV development at WatchUp after the specialist departed)
concerns: C++ proficiency is negligible for a role where it is the core technical requirement — only ~6 months of documented C/C++ work in over 15 years
No industrial automation, IoT, or OT/ICS domain experience and no exposure to relevant protocols (MQTT, OPC UA, Modbus, IEC 61850, DNP 3.0)
No evidence of secure development lifecycle (SDL), IEC 62443, or any industrial cybersecurity practices
